name: New England Baked Plum Pudding
recipeInstructions:
Info: from Early American Recipes collection, 1953,
by Heloise Frost from Nana Swain
1. Soak rolled crackers in milk for at least two hours.
2. In a large bowl, mix together sugar, molasses, spices, salt, cinnamon, clove, and soda.
3. Add beaten eggs to the mixture, then stir in the raisins, currants, citron, orange peel, and lemon peel.
4. Stir in the melted shortening until well combined.
5. Pour the batter into a well-buttered earthen pudding pan.
6. Bake at 400°F (200°C) for one hour.
7. Reduce the temperature to 300°F (150°C) and continue baking for two hours, stirring the pudding thoroughly.
8. Return the pudding to the oven and bake an additional hour or until firm.
9. Run a knife around the edges of the pan, but do not remove the pudding from the pan until it is completely cold.
10. Slice and serve with hard sauce (or whipped cream). The pudding will keep in the refrigerator for a long time.
